steps.name: Overlay data model and pure functions
steps.description: Create overlay.rs with algebraic data type (visible, title, input, items, cursor, width, max_visible_items, scroll_offset) and pure functions: create/reset, set-input, set-items, get-selected, cursor-up/down with clamping. Register module in lib.rs. Add overlay field to EditorState.
criteria: New overlay value created with visible=false and empty collections
Setting input text produces new overlay with updated input
Setting items produces new overlay with updated item list
Get-selected returns item at cursor index or nothing when empty
Cursor-down clamps at last item; cursor-up clamps at zero

steps.name: Overlay bridge primitives (minimal set)
steps.description: Register 5 bridge primitives in bridge.rs: open-overlay, close-overlay, overlay-set-input, overlay-set-items, overlay-get-selected. Each primitive reads/writes the overlay field on EditorState through the existing bridge dispatch pattern.
criteria: open-overlay sets visible=true and resets input/items/cursor
close-overlay sets visible=false
overlay-set-input updates the input text in overlay state
overlay-set-items replaces the items list in overlay state
overlay-get-selected returns the highlighted item string

steps.name: Overlay rendering and cursor override
steps.description: After all existing rendering, check overlay visibility. If visible, draw centered bordered box with input line (prompt + query) and item list with cursor highlight. Override terminal cursor position to end of overlay input when visible.
criteria: Overlay renders centered over editor content when visible
Input line shows prompt character and current query text
Highlighted item row renders with distinct (reversed) style
Terminal cursor positioned at end of overlay input text
No overlay rendered when visible=false

steps.name: Overlay search plugin and browse-mode binding with WS E2E test
steps.description: Create overlay-search/init.lisp: on command overlay-file-search, call list-dir-recursive, open-overlay, populate items, switch keymap. Handle char input (append+filter+update), Enter (get-selected, open-file, close-overlay, restore mode), Escape (close-overlay, restore mode). Add Ctrl-p binding in browse-mode. Write WS-1 E2E test.
criteria: Ctrl-p from browser panel opens overlay with file list
Typing characters filters results by substring match
Enter opens the highlighted file in the editor buffer
Escape dismisses overlay without opening a file
WS-1 E2E test passes: Ctrl-p, type fragment, Enter, edit+save

phases.name: Full overlay features -- navigation, filtering, lifecycle

steps.name: Remaining bridge primitives and overlay title
steps.description: Register 5 remaining bridge primitives: overlay-set-title, overlay-get-input, overlay-cursor-down, overlay-cursor-up, overlay-visible?. Update plugin to use overlay-set-title for prompt and cursor primitives for arrow keys.
criteria: overlay-set-title updates the overlay header text
overlay-get-input returns the current query string
overlay-cursor-down/up move highlight with boundary clamping
overlay-visible? returns true when overlay is open
Plugin uses title primitive instead of hardcoded prompt

steps.name: Arrow navigation, cursor reset on query change, and scroll offset
steps.description: Wire Down/Up arrow keys in overlay keymap to cursor-down/cursor-up. Reset cursor to 0 when query text changes. Implement scroll offset so results beyond max_visible_items are reachable. Render visible window based on scroll_offset.
criteria: Down arrow moves highlight to next result in the list
Up arrow moves highlight to previous result in the list
Typing a character after navigating resets highlight to first result
Results beyond max_visible_items are scrollable via arrow keys
Scroll window adjusts when cursor moves past visible bounds

steps.name: Backspace, case-insensitive filtering, and empty-results handling
steps.description: Handle Backspace in overlay keymap: remove last character, re-filter, update overlay. Make substring matching case-insensitive. Handle Enter on empty results (no-op, overlay stays open). Handle Backspace on empty query (no-op).
criteria: Backspace removes last character and results update to match shorter query
Backspace on empty query leaves overlay open without crash
Typing uppercase query matches lowercase file paths
Enter on empty result list does not open a file or crash
Rapid type-delete-retype shows results matching final query

steps.name: Lifecycle edge cases and target/ exclusion
steps.description: Ensure overlay state resets fully on re-open (no stale query/items). Handle Ctrl-p while overlay already open (no-op or re-open). Filter out target/ directory from file list. Prevent typed characters from leaking to editor buffer on dismiss.
criteria: Re-opening overlay after Escape shows clean state with no stale query
Ctrl-p while overlay is open does not crash the editor
Files inside target/ directory are excluded from search results
Characters typed in overlay do not appear in editor buffer after dismiss
Overlay search skips .git/ and other build artifact directories

phases.name: Browser integration, multi-mode support, and E2E coverage

steps.name: Ctrl-p from normal mode and browser panel navigation
steps.description: Add Ctrl-p binding in normal-mode keymap. After file selection, update browser-current-dir to the opened file's parent directory. Ensure mode restores correctly to browse-mode or normal-mode depending on origin.
criteria: Ctrl-p from normal mode (file editing) opens the search overlay
After file selection, browser panel shows the file's parent directory
Escape from overlay returns to the originating mode (browse or normal)
Mode is normal after file selection regardless of origin
Browser panel remains visible underneath the overlay

steps.name: Regression guards and file-switch robustness
steps.description: Verify overlay works across consecutive file switches. Verify display renders correctly when switching between files of different sizes (gutter width changes). Verify editor commands work normally after overlay selection.
criteria: Opening a second file via overlay after the first works correctly
Switching from large to small file via overlay has no display corruption
Normal-mode commands (i, A, dd) work after overlay file selection
Editor content matches selected file after each overlay search
Overlay search works after :q and reopen cycle

steps.name: Complete E2E test coverage for all milestones
steps.description: Write remaining E2E tests covering all 28 acceptance scenarios: lifecycle (M1-1 through M1-7), search/filtering (M2-1 through M2-8), navigation (M3-1 through M3-6), and browser integration (M4-1 through M4-6). Batch tests by class matching test-scenarios.md structure.
criteria: All WS and M1 lifecycle tests pass (open, dismiss, re-open, no-crash, no-leak)
All M2 filtering tests pass (substring, backspace, case-insensitive, empty, target/)
All M3 navigation tests pass (down, up, boundary clamp, cursor reset on query change)
All M4 integration tests pass (browser nav, content, mode restore, file switch, display)
make ci-local and make e2e pass with zero failures
